```{r rank-anchor-panels, fig.width=12, fig.height=10.5, out.width='95%'}
    cluster_display_map[as.character(cluster_summary$cluster[1])] <- paste0(
      "Cluster ", cluster_summary$cluster[1], " (more deprived)"
    )
    cluster_display_map[as.character(cluster_summary$cluster[2])] <- paste0(
      "Cluster ", cluster_summary$cluster[2], " (less deprived)"
    )
  }

  cluster_display_levels <- unname(cluster_display_map[cluster_levels_original])
  cluster_palette_display <- setNames(
    cluster_palette[cluster_levels_original],
    cluster_display_levels
  )

  rank_plot_df <- rank_plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      cluster_display = factor(
        cluster_display_map[as.character(cluster)],
        levels = cluster_display_levels
      )
    )

  k_selected <- nlevels(rank_plot_df$cluster_display)
  max_rank_value <- max(rank_plot_df$overall_simd_rank, na.rm = TRUE)

  rank_breaks <- pretty(c(1, max_rank_value))
  rank_breaks <- rank_breaks[rank_breaks >= 1 & rank_breaks <= max_rank_value]

  p_rank <- ggplot(
    rank_plot_df,
    aes(x = cluster_display, y = overall_simd_rank, fill = cluster_display)
  ) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.25, width = 0.9, colour = NA, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.18, outlier.alpha = 0.20, show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_palette_display, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
    scale_y_reverse(
      limits = c(max_rank_value, 1),
      breaks = rank_breaks,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Overall SIMD rank by cluster",
      subtitle = paste0(
        params$target_board,
        " | selected raw-indicator k-means partition (k = ",
        k_selected,
        ")"
      ),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Overall SIMD rank (1 = most deprived)",
      fill = "Cluster"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )

  panel_b_df <- rank_plot_df %>%
    select(DataZone, cluster_display, overall_rank_band, all_of(key_vars)) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(key_vars),
      names_to = "indicator",
      values_to = "value"
    ) %>%
    group_by(overall_rank_band, cluster_display, indicator) %>%
    summarise(
      n = sum(!is.na(value)),
      mean_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      se_value = dplyr::if_else(n > 1, sd_value / sqrt(n), NA_real_),
      t_crit = dplyr::if_else(n > 1, qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1)), NA_real_),
      ci_half = se_value * t_crit,
      ymin = mean_value - ci_half,
      ymax = mean_value + ci_half,
      indicator = factor(
        indicator,
        levels = key_vars,
        labels = unname(indicator_label_map[key_vars])
      )
    )

  panel_caption <- paste0(
    "Relationship between selected cluster membership and overall SIMD rank in ",
    params$target_board,
    ". Panel (a) shows the distribution of overall SIMD rank by cluster for the selected raw-indicator k-means partition (k = ",
    k_selected,
    "). Lower rank values indicate greater deprivation. Panel (b) shows mean indicator values by cluster within broad overall SIMD rank tertiles. ",
    "Error bars indicate 95% confidence intervals. Cluster-by-tertile representation is uneven, especially at the extremes, so those comparisons should be interpreted cautiously."
  )

  knitr::opts_current$set(fig.cap = panel_caption)

  p_band <- ggplot(
    panel_b_df,
    aes(x = overall_rank_band, y = mean_value, colour = cluster_display, group = cluster_display)
  ) +
    geom_errorbar(
      aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
      width = 0.08,
      alpha = 0.6,
      linewidth = 0.65,
      na.rm = TRUE,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = TRUE) +
    geom_point(size = 2, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ indicator, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cluster_palette_display, drop = FALSE, name = "Cluster") +
    labs(
      title = "Domain composition within broad overall-rank bands",
      subtitle = paste0(
        toupper(substr(rank_band_name_plural, 1, 1)),
        substr(rank_band_name_plural, 2, nchar(rank_band_name_plural)),
        " of overall SIMD rank within ", params$target_board,
        " (T1 = most deprived)"
      ),
      x = "Overall SIMD rank band",
      y = "Mean indicator value",
      colour = "Cluster"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  wrap_plots(p_rank, p_band, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 1.55), guides = "collect") +
    plot_annotation(
      title = paste0(
        "Relationship between selected cluster membership and overall SIMD rank in ",
        params$target_board
      ),
      tag_levels = "a"
    ) &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}
```

## Maps for selected clustering results

```{r maps, fig.width=13, fig.height=10}
map_sf <- board_sf_complete %>%
  left_join(cluster_assignments, by = "DataZone")

map_cols <- selected_cluster_cols[grepl("_k[0-9]+$", selected_cluster_cols)]

map_list <- purrr::map(
  map_cols,
  ~ plot_cluster_map(map_sf, .x, title_text = .x)
)

wrap_plots(map_list, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Maps for selected clustering results")
```

## Difference maps

```{r difference-maps, fig.width=13, fig.height=10}
difference_plot_list <- list(
  plot_difference_map(map_sf, raw_kmeans_col, raw_hclust_col, "Raw: K-means vs hierarchical"),
  plot_difference_map(map_sf, pc_kmeans_col, pc_hclust_col, "PC: K-means vs hierarchical"),
  plot_difference_map(map_sf, raw_kmeans_col, pc_kmeans_col, "K-means: raw vs PC"),
  plot_difference_map(map_sf, raw_hclust_col, pc_hclust_col, "Hierarchical: raw vs PC")
)

if (isTRUE(params$use_cmeans)) {
  difference_plot_list <- c(
    difference_plot_list,
    list(
      plot_difference_map(map_sf, raw_cmeans_col, pc_cmeans_col, "C-means: raw vs PC")
    )
  )
}

if (isTRUE(params$use_mclust)) {
  difference_plot_list <- c(
    difference_plot_list,
    list(
      plot_difference_map(map_sf, raw_mclust_col, pc_mclust_col, "Finite mixture model: raw vs PC")
    )
  )
}

wrap_plots(difference_plot_list, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Difference maps for selected clustering comparisons")
```

# Outputs passed to the vine document

```{r export-vine-inputs}
dir.create(params$output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

readr::write_csv(board_complete, file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_analysis_base.csv")))
readr::write_csv(raw_feature_tbl, file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_feature_set_raw.csv")))
readr::write_csv(pc_feature_tbl, file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_feature_set_pc.csv")))
readr::write_csv(cluster_assignments, file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_cluster_assignments.csv")))

if (isTRUE(params$use_cmeans)) {
  readr::write_csv(
    raw_cmeans_membership_export,
    file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_cmeans_memberships_raw.csv"))
  )
  readr::write_csv(
    pc_cmeans_membership_export,
    file.path(params$output_dir, paste0(output_prefix, "_cmeans_memberships_pc.csv"))
  )
}
```

The next document should read:

- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_analysis_base.csv")`
- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_feature_set_raw.csv")`
- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_feature_set_pc.csv")`
- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_cluster_assignments.csv")`

If a reference C-means solution is carried forward as an uncertainty-aware comparator, it should also read:

- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_cmeans_memberships_raw.csv")`
- `r paste0(output_prefix, "_cmeans_memberships_pc.csv")`

These C-means membership files contain:

- `DataZone`
- `cluster_1_membership`
- `cluster_2_membership`
- `max_membership`
- `hard_label`
- `core_borderline_flag`

The chosen clustering result for vine fitting is then selected by its column name, for example:

- `raw_kmeans_k2`
- `pc_hclust_k2`
- `raw_cmeans_k2`
- `pc_mclust_k2`

A useful next-stage sensitivity check is to fit vines on **all members** first and then refit on a stricter **core-only subset** defined from `max_membership` (for example `r paste0(">= ", params$vine_core_membership_threshold)`) for the reference C-means solution. The updated vine document supports this directly through optional C-means membership inputs and a core-membership threshold parameter.

# Session info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
